feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online – Certificate-Based OAuth2 Client Credentials
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently implementing an integration between SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) and Microsoft SharePoint Online.

The SharePoint team has confirmed the following requirements:

  • Direct Client Certificate Authentication (mTLS) is not supported.
  • Authentication must be performed via Microsoft Entra ID App Registration.
  • Client Secret authentication is not preferred due to password expiry and security requirements.
  • Certificate-Based OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication is the recommended approach.

While evaluating the standard authentication options available in SAP CPI, we could not identify an out-of-the-box configuration for OAuth2 Client Credentials using certificate-based authentication (Private Key JWT / JWT Assertion) by leveraging an existing CPI Key Pair (certificate and private key).

The options currently available in our CPI tenant are:

  • OAuth2 Client Credentials
  • OAuth2 Authorization Code
  • OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion
  • Client Certificate Authentication

However, we could not find a standard way to configure OAuth2 Client Credentials with certificate/private key mapping for Microsoft Entra ID without implementing custom JWT generation logic through Groovy scripting.

I would appreciate feedback from anyone who has worked on a similar scenario.

Questions:

  1. Has anyone successfully implemented SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online integration using Microsoft Entra ID App Registration and certificate-based OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication?

  2. Were you able to achieve this using only standard CPI capabilities?

  3. If custom development was required, did you implement JWT generation through Groovy scripting or use an alternative approach?

  4. Has SAP provided any standard capability, roadmap item, SAP Note, or recommended approach for this authentication pattern?

  5. What is the generally accepted best practice for SAP CPI integrations where Microsoft Entra ID requires certificate-based authentication instead of client secret authentication?

Any guidance, implementation experience, SAP Notes, blogs, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: upgrade Flexso TDD integration suite environment to enhanced edition
entry.content:
We would like to upgrade our TDD Integration Suite environment to "enhanced edition", in order to get hands-on access to new MCP gateway component. 
What is the procedure to get this going?

entry.title: Change color of DEV, QAS & PRD BTP's Integration Suite portals
entry.content:
Hello SAP BTP support,

Is there a way to change colors of our BTP IS environments in a web-browser? It is very confusing to have all of them identical as many people make mistakes when working with 2 or all 3 environments. 

Please let me know how can I change color?

Many thanks,,

MAC

entry.title: Looking for ODATA API from SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
Dear SAP, 

We are looking to GET these fields as part of ODATA API from SuccessFactors LMS System.

Here are the fields.

SFUserAssessmentData
    
                        name=UserID
                             name=ItemID
                            name=Iteration
                            name=LastAccessDate
                             name=Finished
                           name=Complete
                            name=CompletionDate
                            name=QuestionsAsked
                             name=QuestionsCorrect
                            name=Evaluation
                          name=ExamEvaluation

 

Let us know the ODATA API to be used in pulling these data from SF LMS.

entry.title: Inquiry about boolean value support in SAP CPI for Regex Filtering setting
entry.content: I am trying to understand why setting the Regex Filtering to true in SAP CPI does not work, but setting it to 1 works. I want to know if SAP CPI supports boolean values as true or false or if it accepts 1/0. I observed that when I set to true, it didn't pick the file, but when I set it to 1, it started working. I need clarification on supported boolean value formats in SAP CPI for this setting.

entry.title: Maximum Pool Size 10までの制限について
entry.content:
以下のサイトに記載されておりますコネクションプールの設定について確認させて下さい。

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/managing-jdbc-data-sources?utm_source=chatgpt.com&locale=en-US

上記サイトには以下のような記載があるのですが、

Maximum Pool Size

The upper limit on the number of database connections maintained by the connection pool, including active and idle connections.

It controls peak resource usage and prevents database server overload. Default value: 10 Valid range: 1 to database-supported connection limit

POCのExit Criteriaを満たす上でMaximum Po

entry.title: Is this a known limitation or defect in Trial tenants?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am working with SAP Integration Suite Trial (Cloud Integration v8.44.19) and trying to use the native Git integration for my Integration Flow artifacts.

I have been investigating this issue for quite some time and have performed extensive root cause analysis before posting here.

Environment

  • SAP Integration Suite Trial
  • Cloud Integration Version: 8.44.19
  • GitHub Repository: Public
  • GitHub Personal Access Token: Fine-grained PAT
  • CI/CD Service: Subscribed and role collections assigned
  • Package: Created from scratch (not imported from ZIP)
  • Integration Flow: Created from scratch

    Problem

    Whenever I perform Git Push, I immediately receive the following backend response:

     

     
    {
      "errorCode": "REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND",
      "errorMessage": "The artifact child doesn't contain the repository information"
    }
     Sometimes I also receive:

     

     
    This artifact is not associated with a Git repository.
    Git operations can't be performed.
     

    What I have already verified

    GitHub

    • Repository exists.
    • Repository is accessible.
    • Repository is public.
    • PAT is valid.
    • PAT has the required permissions.

      SAP Git Access

      Git Access configuration is successful.

      When opening Git Push:

      • Repository dropdown is populated correctly.
      • Selecting the repository automatically loads the branches.
      • Branch selection works correctly.

        Therefore, GitHub connectivity appears to be working.

        Browser Developer Tools

        Using Chrome Developer Tools (Network tab), I observed:

        • Repository list is fetched successfully.
        • Branch list is fetched successfully.
        • Push request immediately fails with

           

           
          REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND
           UI Investigation

          While debugging the SAP UI JavaScript, I noticed the Git Push controller expects repository metadata through:

           

           
          this.repoDetails.repoName
          this.repoDetails.repoURL
          this.repoDetails.branch
           The controller also validates:

           

           
          if (!oModelData.repoName ||
              !oModelData.repoURL ||
              !oModelData.branch)
          {
              sap.m.MessageToast.show("repository configuration not found");
          }
           This suggests the frontend expects repository metadata to already exist for the artifact.

          Previous errors observed

          During earlier troubleshooting I also encountered:

           

           
          GIT_OPERATION_FORBIDDEN
           
           
          repository configuration not found
           
           
          This artifact is not associated with a Git repository.
           Eventually the backend consistently reports:

           

           
          REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND
           Root Cause Analysis Performed

          Based on my investigation, I believe the following have already been ruled out:

          • GitHub connectivity
          • Personal Access Token
          • Repository visibility
          • Branch availability
          • Repository configuration
          • Package creation from ZIP
          • Imported artifacts

            The repository and branch can both be fetched successfully, which suggests GitHub communication is functioning correctly.

            The failure appears to occur because the backend cannot find Git repository metadata associated with the Integration Flow artifact.

            My Question

            Has anyone experienced this issue on SAP Integration Suite Trial?

            Specifically:

            1. Is this a known limitation or defect in Trial tenants?
            2. How is the repository metadata associated with an Integration Flow/package?
            3. Is there any supported way to recreate or repair the missing repository association?
            4. Has anyone successfully resolved REPOSITORY_INFORMATION_NOT_FOUND for Git Push in Cloud Integration Trial?

              Any guidance or references to SAP Notes or official documentation would be greatly appreciated.

              Thank you.

entry.title: how to read SAP Application Message Type in integration
entry.content:
Hello all,

I'm working in an integration where I need to read the property "SAP Application Message Type" to apply its value in a router.

I've set the condition as "${header.SAP_MessageType}" but it's not working. Could you help to clarify this, please?

Thanks!

entry.title: Can SAP API Management directly invoke SAP S/4HANA OData services without SAP Cloud Integration?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an architectural question regarding the use of SAP API Management and would appreciate your insights.

Scenario

I have a client application that sends data to an API Proxy in SAP API Management. Instead of forwarding the request to an SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) iFlow, I am considering calling an SAP S/4HANA OData service directly from the API Proxy.

Since the target OData service requires an X-CSRF token for POST operations, the API Proxy would need to perform the following steps:

  1. Receive the incoming request from the client.
  2. Fetch the X-CSRF token from the SAP S/4HANA OData service.
  3. Reuse the token (and cookies, if required) to execute the POST request.
  4. Return the response back to the client.

My Questions

  1. Is this architecture technically feasible using only SAP API Management, without involving SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)?
  2. Can SAP API Management reliably handle the complete X-CSRF token flow (token retrieval, cookie handling, and POST request)?
  3. Is this approach recommended for production environments, or are there any technical limitations or best practices that should be considered?
  4. What would be the major drawbacks compared to using SAP Cloud Integration, particularly regarding:
    • Error handling
    • Retry mechanisms
    • Monitoring and logging
    • Security
    • Scalability
    • Maintainability

Why I'm Asking

The motivation behind this approach is to optimize SAP Cloud Integration message consumption and potentially reduce licensing costs for simple pass-through scenarios. If API Management can securely and reliably handle such integrations, it could eliminate the need for an iFlow in certain use cases.

Has anyone implemented a similar architecture in a production environment? If so, I would appreciate hearing about your experience and whether you would recommend this approach.

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

entry.title: Request for Clarification: HTTP Adapter Retry Configuration
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

SAP previously informed us that the retry functionality can only be applied at the adapter level and cannot be enabled individually for specific programs.

After applying the patch, when we add a new HTTP Adapter within the current IS program, a Retry tab is now available.

Currently, the Retry on Exception and Retry on Error Response options are disabled.

Could you please confirm whether enabling these options would allow the retry functionality to be applied only to the specific program, or whether it would affect all HTTP Adapters across the system?

 

 

[05/July/2026 Monthly Patch description]

Cloud Integration－HTTP Receiver Adapter Retry Configuration Enhancements Available Now

You can now access and manage enhanced retry configurations for the HTTP Receiver Adapter through the Retry tab.

  • Retry on Exception and its related options are now available in the Retry tab.

  • Retry Failed Request f

entry.title: Supported Ways to Transfer AL11 Files from S/4HANA RISE → BTP CPI
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

would like to have an connectivity to pick files from S4 AL11 directories into BTP CPI .

Currently we are in the phase of migrating from PI/PO to BTP CPI as part of initial prerequisites 

we need to integrate S4hana AL11 directories with BTP CPI 

as such there is no file adapter available in cpi 

So please guide or suggest the steps or process do implement and get it done .

Regards

Vidya Sagar

entry.title: Posting Key Issue in FB01 and BAPI
entry.content:
• When posting a customer to bank entry in FB01, we input posting key 15, and the system correctly accepts and posts using this key.

  • However, when performing the same posting through the BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST function module with the posting key set to 15, the system ignores the input and instead uses posting key 11 automatically.

entry.title: OAuth token auto retrieval not working in IFLOW
entry.content:
Hi,

We are working on an Interface development to integrate to an external system using HTTP(REST url) from CPI.

SAP S4(SOAP) -> CPI (HTTP - OAUTH)-> Layer7 - > NS Application

This connection is established using OAUTH credentials in the security material in our CPI tenant below. 

Login

When the interface is triggered from SAP S4 we are encountering an issue "Service not available", on analyzing the issue, an workaround is implemented in the IFLOW with a groovy script to fetch the OAUTH token manually. This work around was successful. we would like to get it clarified from SAP team, IF CPI IFLOW will support automatic token fetch or we need to implement the groovy to fetch the token. 

 

Thanks

entry.title: where to generate the SSH key pair for SFTP adapter in EIC with private key authentication
entry.content: I am deploying an iFlow on EIC with both source and target systems using SFTP adapters with private key authentication. I want to confirm whether the key pair should be generated on EIC or CPI, and what configuration steps are required on EIC and CPI. I need guidance on the proper setup process for private key authentication in this scenario.

entry.title: EDI Integration Templates for SAP Integration Advisor have mode configure-only
entry.content:
Hi!!!

We need to use the following template in Discover (Integrations) in Integration Suite:

The EDI integration templates for SAP Integration Advisor are read-only, and we cannot change anything.

Thank you

entry.title: Building an Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot: A Practical Approach to Intelligent SAP Support
entry.content:
Every SAP implementation eventually reaches the same stage Production Support. As organizations grow, support teams receive hundreds of requests every month. Many of these requests are repetitive and are not caused by system defects, but by business process misunderstandings, authorization issues, workflow delays, master data validations, or integration failures.
Typical examples include:

  • Unable to create a Purchase Order
  • Invoice posting failures
  • Sales Orders blocked
  • Workflow approvals pending
  • User authorization issues
  • SAP Integration Suite interface failures
  • Background job failures

Although these issues are common, users still need to create support tickets and wait for the support team to investigate.

This made me think differently.

What if every SAP user had an intelligent assistant that could understand the business process, analyze the issue, and provide the right guidance before a support ticket was ever created.

This idea led to the design of an Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot.

The objective was simple:

  • Reduce repetitive support tickets.
  • Provide immediate guidance to business users.
  • Improve user experience.
  • Allow SAP support teams to focus on complex business problems rather than repetitive operational issues.

Solution Overview:
The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot is designed on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
Instead of acting as a simple chatbot, it combines enterprise knowledge, live SAP data, business process understanding, and AI reasoning.
When a business user asks a question, the Copilot:

  • Understands the SAP process.
  • Retrieves enterprise knowledge.
  • Validates live SAP information.
  • Determines the probable root cause.
  • Suggests the appropriate resolution.
  • Automatically escalates the issue when technical intervention is required.

This creates a much faster and more intelligent support experience.

Architecture Overview:How the Solution Works

Step 1 – User Raises a Question
Instead of creating a support ticket, the business user simply asks the AI Copilot.

Example: Why can’t I create Purchase Order 4XXXXXXXXX?
The Copilot immediately begins analyzing the request. 

Step 2 – Understanding the Business Context
The first responsibility of the Copilot is to understand what the user is asking.
It identifies:

  • SAP Module
  • Business Process
  • Business Object
  • User Context

In this example, it identifies:

Module: Procurement
Business Object: Purchase Order

Step 3 – Searching Enterprise Knowledge

The Copilot searches enterprise knowledge that has been prepared by the SAP project team.
This knowledge includes:

  • Business Process Documents
  • Functional Specifications
  • Standard Operating Procedures
  • Configuration Documents
  • SAP Notes
  • Previous Incident Resolutions
  • Integration Documentation
  • Support Runbooks
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Unlike traditional chatbots, the Copilot searches only trusted enterprise knowledge.

Step 4 – Validating Live SAP Data
The next step is validating the issue against the SAP system.
Using SAP APIs, the Copilot performs live validations.

For a Purchase Order issue, it checks:

  • Vendor Status
  • Purchasing Organization
  • Material Availability
  • User Authorization
  • Approval Workflow
  • Master Data Validation
  • Related Integration Status

This allows the AI to understand the actual business situation rather than simply searching documentation.

Step 5 – AI Reasoning
After collecting information from SAP and the enterprise knowledge repository, the AI determines the root cause.For example, suppose the Vendor has been blocked.

Instead of showing a technical SAP error message, the Copilot explains:

Vendor 1XXXX is blocked for Purchasing Organization 1XXX. This is a business restriction rather than a system issue. Please submit a Vendor Activation Request using the Procurement workflow.

The user immediately understands:

  • Why the issue occurred.
  • Who owns the issue.
  • What action needs to be taken.

No support ticket is required.

 Why This Solution Is Different:

Traditional chatbots simply search documents. The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot goes much further.

It combines:

  • Enterprise Knowledge
  • SAP Business Processes
  • Live SAP Data
  • AI Reasoning
  • Operational Knowledge

This enables the Copilot to provide meaningful business guidance rather than generic answers.

Business Benefits:

The solution can provide significant operational benefits.

  • Faster resolution of common SAP issues.
  • Reduced repetitive support tickets.
  • Improved first-time resolution.
  • Better employee experience.
  • Consistent troubleshooting across SAP modules.
  • Reduced investigation effort for SAP support teams.
  • Better utilization of SAP functional and technical consultants.
  • Enterprise knowledge becomes available whenever it is needed. 

Conclusion:

Artificial Intelligence should not replace SAP consultants.Instead, it should make enterprise knowledge available to every business user at the moment they need it.The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot demonstrates how SAP Business Technology Platform, enterprise knowledge, and AI can work together to transform traditional support into an intelligent digital experience.

Rather than waiting for answers, users receive immediate guidance.SAP support teams can focus on innovation and business transformation.As organizations continue their SAP modernization journey, combining AI with enterprise business knowledge presents a practical opportunity to improve user experience, increase operational efficiency, and build a smarter support organization.

entry.title: pick file from AL11 through CPI and send to external SFTP server
entry.content: pick file from AL11 through CPI and send to external SFTP server

entry.title: FTP ISSUE FOR BIDDEN
entry.content: while connectivity FTP, getting a forbidden issue

entry.title: Request for information for SAP Intgeration Suite
entry.content:
Hi, we are using SAP Integration Suite to devlopp mutilple Flow and we have some questions :

Concurrency & Threading

 

What is the maximum number of concurrent iFlow executions (worker threads) allowed per tenant on the Integration Suite Starter/Edge plan? Is this configurable or fixed?

When multiple instances of the same iFlow run in parallel, are they queued, rejected, or executed with degraded performance? What is the exact queuing/throttling behavior?

Does concurrent execution of the same iFlow share any runtime resources (JVM heap, thread pool, Camel routes) that could cause performance degradation as concurrency increases?

 

HTTP/Message Processing Timeouts

 

What is the maximum synchronous HTTP request processing timeout enforced by the Integration Suite runtime (Cloud Foundry router + CPI runtime) for a single message exchange? Is this configurable at the iFlow, tenant, or platform level?

Is there a difference in timeout behavior between the HTTPS Sender adapter and other trigger types (e.g., timer-based, JMS-based)?

If a message exceeds the timeout mid-processing, is there a way to receive a partial-comp

entry.title: Testing SAP standard API on RISE backend
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When testing SAP Standard OData APIs on SAP RISE, who in your team owns and maintains the Postman collection?

Who should provide the following? 

 1. OAuth 2.0 setup for IAS

 2. Environment variables for Dev/QA/Prod

 3. Example requests for all CRUD operations

It’s working well, but before we roll this out company-wide I’d like feedback:

1. Is there an SAP recommended way to package + version Postman collections?

2. Any security concerns sharing collections with tokens in them?

3. Does SAP plan to publish official Postman collections for all S/4HANA Cloud APIs?

4. Could you please guide me to setup postman collections?

Regards, 

Ramu.
